stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers, llama, and text-generation.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking of a significant LLM model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model from Meta.
It is featured on the Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating community interest and performance tracking.
The associated tags confirm its relevance to transformer models and text generation tasks.

stories.title: Google invests in A24 to build AI movie tools
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Google's DeepMind is partnering with A24 to develop AI tools for film production.
The collaboration aims to enhance filmmakers' storytelling capabilities using AI technology.
Google is reportedly investing approximately $75 million into A24 as part of the R&D effort.

stories.title: Actionable Activation Directions for Detecting and Mitigating Emergent Misalignment Across Language Model Families
stories.author: Abdul Rafay Syed
bullet_points: The research identifies that fine-tuning LLMs on insecure code can cause 'emergent misalignment' due to poorly understood internal model structures.
The study proposes and validates an 'actionable activation-space direction' that can causally separate aligned from misaligned model states.
This direction achieved extremely high separation (99.6%) across four distinct, instruction-tuned model families (Qwen2.5, Gemma-2, Llama-3.2, Ministral-3).

stories.title: Moebius: 0.2B image inpainting model with 10B-level performance
stories.author: DSemba
bullet_points: Introduction of 'Moebius,' a new image inpainting model.
The model is notable for achieving 10B-level performance despite having only 0.2B parameters.
This suggests significant efficiency gains in generative AI models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text generation tasks, indicating its application in conversational AI.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline demonstrated is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in the MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher output quality, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads on Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The core value proposition is running AI entirely on the user's own device for privacy.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It supports running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) using both local and cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: NHTSA investigating alleged Tesla Autopilot crash that killed woman in her home
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: NHTSA is investigating a fatal crash involving a Tesla using Autopilot.
The incident occurred while the woman was in her home, raising questions about system usage.
The report highlights the conflict between Tesla's marketing of Autopilot as 'lifesaving' and the real-world fatality.

stories.title: How Anthropic may have talked itself into an AI export ban
stories.author: Clara Murray, Financial Times
bullet_points: Anthropic's public warnings about advanced AI dangers are reportedly more pronounced than those from OpenAI.
The article speculates that Anthropic's rhetoric may be influencing or leading to potential AI export restrictions.
This highlights a growing divergence in how major AI labs are framing the risks associated with frontier AI models.

stories.title: The UK will scan asylum-seekers’ faces for age checks—despite knowing the tech is flawed
stories.author: Matt Burgess, Maddy Varner, May Bulman, Gabriel Geiger, WIRED.com
bullet_points: The UK government plans to use facial recognition technology to verify the age of asylum seekers.
The technology has been tested and shown to carry risks of significant, life-altering errors.
The decision to proceed with the flawed technology highlights a conflict between technological implementation and accuracy/human rights.

stories.title: FlowEdit: Associative Memory for Lifelong Pronunciation Adaptation in Flow-Matching TTS
stories.author: Nityanand Mathur
bullet_points: FlowEdit addresses a key limitation in state-of-the-art flow-matching TTS: the inability to adapt to new pronunciations (like OOV proper nouns) without full retraining.
It introduces a lifelong adaptation framework that learns pronunciation corrections by applying latent conditioning edits rather than modifying model weights.
The method optimizes token-level perturbations within the text embedding space when corrective feedback is supplied, enabling efficient, continuous learning.

stories.title: StylisticBias: A Few Human Visual Cues Drive Most Social Biases in MLLMs
stories.author: Jana Diesner
bullet_points: Introduces 'StylisticBias,' a new controlled benchmark for evaluating attribute-level social bias in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
Addresses the critical gap in understanding how visual cues influence MLLMs' judgments of people, separating appearance effects from identity differences.
The research uses 500 photorealistic base faces to systematically test and quantify social biases within these increasingly deployed AI systems.

stories.title: Your Mouse and Eyes Secretly Leak Your Preference: LLM Alignment using Implicit Feedback from Users
stories.author: Hamed Zamani
bullet_points: The paper proposes a new LLM alignment method that utilizes implicit user feedback instead of relying solely on expensive explicit human feedback.
Existing alignment methods are limited by the scarcity and high cost of collecting explicit human preference annotations.
The research aims to leverage implicit signals, similar to those used by major internet platforms, to improve LLM alignment efficiency.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Valve is working with AMD to bring FSR 4 to the Steam Machine
stories.author: Cameron Faulkner
bullet_points: Valve is collaborating with AMD to integrate FSR 4 technology into the Steam Machine.
The Steam Machine is noted as having performance comparable to a PlayStation 5.
The current implementation of AMD FSR on the Steam Machine is considered suboptimal and needs improvement.

stories.title: Valve will finally let you build your own Steam Machine with SteamOS for desktop
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Valve is enabling users to build their own Steam Machines using custom PC parts.
This functionality is being introduced starting with the SteamOS 3.8 release.
The update also includes general improvements, such as enhanced compatibility.

stories.title: A US military exercise in space got underway with barely anyone noticing
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: The US Space Force is focused on drastically accelerating satellite deployment timelines.
The goal is to reduce the time from years to mere weeks, days, or even hours.
The exercise highlights the military's push for rapid modernization of space capabilities.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The paper investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM utilizing a continuous latent space for computation.
Model reasoning transparency is highlighted as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment in LLMs.
Transparency is decomposed into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and algorithmic aspects.

stories.title: How Do Instructions Shape Speech? Cross-Attention Attribution for Style-Captioned Text-to-Speech
stories.author: Sudarshan Kamath
bullet_points: The research addresses the lack of clarity regarding how individual words influence the acoustic output in style-captioned Text-to-Speech (TTS) systems.
A novel method, cross-attention attribution, is proposed and adapted from the DAAM framework for speech diffusion models.
The technique generates per-token heatmaps across multiple layers to improve the controllability and diagnose failure modes in expressive TTS.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework designed to give AI agents structured state management for tool-calling.
Addresses a key limitation in standard agents where task state (facts, constraints, conditions) must be reconstructed from the prompt in every turn.
Focuses on improving policy adherence and state maintenance for complex, multi-turn applications like customer service agents.

stories.title: DeepSWIP: Quotient-WMC Counterfactuals for Neural Probabilistic Logic Programs
stories.author: Fengxiang He
bullet_points: Introduces DeepSWIP, a new framework for counterfactual reasoning in neurosymbolic systems like DeepProbLog.
Addresses the limitation of standard associational inference by providing a single-world counterfactual semantics.
Uses neural materialization to translate fixed-context neural predicates into standard ProbLog choices for intervention computation.

stories.title: SARLO-80: Worldwide Slant SAR Language Optic Dataset 80cm
stories.author: Georgia Channing
bullet_points: SARLO-80 introduces a new, large-scale dataset designed to bridge the gap between optical and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) data.
The dataset addresses limitations in existing SAR-optical resources, which often lack complex-valued measurements and native acquisition geometry.
This development is crucial for advancing physically grounded multimodal foundation models in remote sensing and Earth observation.

stories.title: Multi-LCB: Extending LiveCodeBench to Multiple Programming Languages
stories.author: Dmitrii Babaev
bullet_points: The paper introduces Multi-LCB, an extension of LiveCodeBench (LCB) to support multiple programming languages.
LCB is a recognized benchmark for evaluating LLMs on code generation, known for its contamination-aware design.
The research addresses a limitation of LCB, which was previously restricted only to Python, aiming to test cross-language generalization of LLMs.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The research investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM utilizing a continuous latent space for computation.
Model reasoning transparency is highlighted as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment in LLMs.
The study decomposes transparency into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and another component (implied).

stories.title: Predictability as a Fine-Grained Measure for Privacy
stories.author: Karthik Sridharan
bullet_points: Introduces 'privacy via predictability' as a fine-grained alternative to traditional Differential Privacy (DP).
Addresses the costly privacy-accuracy tradeoff inherent in worst-case DP guarantees.
Explicitly models privacy leakage by incorporating attacker knowledge, compromised data, and query types.

stories.title: Execution-State Capsules: Graph-Bound Execution-State Checkpoint and Restore for Low-Latency, Small-Batch, On-Device Physical-AI Serving
stories.author: Liang Su
bullet_points: Addresses a gap in current LLM serving by focusing on low-latency, small-batch, on-device scenarios, unlike high-throughput systems.
Introduces 'Execution-State Capsules' for checkpointing and restoring the full execution state, not just the KV cache.
Targets use cases like interactive LLM agents, speech systems, and robot policies that require frequent branching and tight responsiveness.

stories.title: Probe-and-Refine Tuning of Repository Guidance for Coding Agents
stories.author: Jeannie Albrecht
bullet_points: The research addresses a key limitation of LLM coding agents: their lack of high-level operational knowledge about a software repository.
The paper investigates the effectiveness of using external context files (like AGENTS.md) to guide coding agents.
It proposes a 'Probe-and-Refine' tuning method to improve repository guidance, addressing conflicting prior findings on context utility.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework for maintaining structured task states in AI agents.
Addresses a limitation in standard agents where state must be reconstructed from the prompt across turns.
Focuses on enabling policy-adherent tool-calling agents, particularly relevant for customer service domains.

stories.title: Scalable Training of Spatially Grounded 2D Vision-Language Models for Radiology
stories.author: Thomas Brox
bullet_points: Introduces RefRad2D, a large-scale, bilingual (German/English) dataset of 1.2M CT/MR image-text pairs for radiology.
Presents RefRad2D's creation via automated curation and LLM-based generation, eliminating the need for manual spatial annotations.
Develops RadGrounder, a VLM capable of joint tasks including report generation, visual question answering, and spatial grounding in medical imaging.

stories.title: CATCH-ME if you RAG: a dataset of Contextually Annotated multi-Turn Counterspeech against Hate and Misinformation Exchanges
stories.author: Marco Guerini
bullet_points: Addresses the overlap between hate speech and misinformation, a common challenge in online content moderation.
Proposes a new dataset ('CATCH-ME') to provide high-quality, contextually annotated examples for generating effective counterspeech.
Aims to improve LLM performance in generating non-repetitive and specific responses against harmful online exchanges.

stories.title: Token-Operations-Oriented Inference Optimization Techniques for Large Models
stories.author: Qinghuai Ma
bullet_points: The paper focuses on token-oriented inference optimization, a critical area for making large models scalable and cost-effective.
It introduces a novel four-layer technical architecture: Multi-model Fusion, Model Optimization, Compute-Model Fusion, and Compute-Network-Model Fusion.
The research provides a systematic review of current key technologies and industry status across these optimization levels.

stories.title: PsyScore: A Psychometrically-Aware Framework for Trait-Adaptive Essay Scoring and ZPD-Scaffolded Feedback
stories.author: Chanjin Zheng
bullet_points: Introduces PsyScore, a framework designed to integrate trait-adaptive essay scoring with instructional feedback.
Addresses limitations in current Automated Essay Scoring (AES) by combining psychometric awareness with LLM capabilities.
Aims to move beyond fragmented systems where scoring and feedback are treated separately, offering more actionable insights.

stories.title: The Register Gap: A Meaning Intelligence Framework for Nigerian Public Discourse
stories.author: Celestine Achi
bullet_points: Introduces the Meaning Intelligence Framework (MIF) for analyzing Nigerian public discourse.
MIF is a nine-dimension schema designed to differentiate true communicative intent from surface-level sentiment.
The research posits that AI failure in Nigerian discourse stems from context failure, not just translation issues, challenging existing three-way sentiment benchmarks.

stories.title: The AI Security Gap: Why your autonomous agents are completely unprotected
stories.author: magopredator
bullet_points: Focuses on the critical security vulnerabilities of autonomous AI agents.
Highlights the existence of a significant 'AI Security Gap' in current deployments.
Suggests the need for enhanced protection mechanisms for autonomous systems.

stories.title: What Happens When We Stop Asking
stories.author: eayurt
bullet_points: Stack Overflow usage is projected to drastically decline, from 200k questions/month in 2014 to 3k in 2026.
The primary driver for this decline is the increasing adoption and capability of Artificial Intelligence (AI).
The author expresses concern that the implications of this shift extend beyond just Q&A platforms.
